title: Body Parsing, Raw Webhooks, Forms, Multipart Uploads, and Byte Limits · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but broken bounded body ownership and parser matrix; preserve the failing exchange, locate the first causal Node or Express boundary, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded body ownership and parser matrix and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure express.json type, limit, inflate, strict, reviver, and verify behavior while treating req.body as undefined until parsing succeeds.
Enforce declared, compressed, decoded, field-count, nesting, and semantic-size budgets before expensive parsing or effects.
Preserve exact raw bytes for signature verification while separating timestamp, replay, media type, decode, validation, and enqueue decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Inspect the failure record · Debug Express Json Limit Inflate Verify
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team handles case 2085 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound declared and decoded bodies while preserving exact signed webhook bytes; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure express.json type, limit, inflate, strict, reviver, and verify behavior while treating req.body as undefined until parsing succeeds.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stop reads and downstream work on abort, remove partial artifacts, record bounded causal evidence, and test slow, truncated, malformed, oversized, and changed bodies.

hints: For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Body Abort Cleanup Evidence, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Body Abort Cleanup Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Body Abort Cleanup Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team handles case 1512 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound declared and decoded bodies while preserving exact signed webhook bytes; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Stop reads and downstream work on abort, remove partial artifacts, record bounded causal evidence, and test slow, truncated, malformed, oversized, and changed bodies.

For Node and Express server Publish the incident record · Debug Body Abort Cleanup Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: When the client disconnects, Node automatically rolls back every application effect and removes temporary files.
